question_blocks: ["{"type": "text", "body": "Which of the following are advantages of Ridge Regression?"}"]
options: ["Reduces overfitting","Handles multicollinearity","Eliminates all features completely","Improves model generalization"]
explanation: Ridge Regression adds an L2 penalty term to reduce model complexity. It helps control overfitting and improves generalization on unseen data. It is also useful when predictor variables are highly correlated. However, unlike Lasso Regression, it does not eliminate coefficients completely.

question_blocks: ["{"type": "text", "body": "Select all assumptions commonly associated with linear regression."}"]
explanation: Linear regression assumes a linear relationship between predictors and the target variable. Residuals should be independent and have constant variance. These assumptions help ensure reliable statistical inference. A categorical target variable is generally handled using logistic regression instead.

question_blocks: ["{"type": "text", "body": "A logistic regression model predicts a probability of 0.92 for class 1. Using a threshold of 0.5, determine the predicted class."}"]
explanation: In logistic regression, predicted probabilities greater than the threshold are assigned to the positive class. Since 0.92 is greater than 0.5, the predicted class is 1. Threshold-based classification is a standard approach in binary classification. Different thresholds may be selected depending on application needs.
